questions.question: What does marginalisation mean?
questions.correct_answer: Marginalisation means being pushed to the edge of society and having limited access to resources and opportunities.
questions.explanation: Certain groups may face social, economic or political disadvantages.

questions.question: Who are Adivasis?
questions.correct_answer: Adivasis are indigenous tribal communities who traditionally live in forest and hill regions.
questions.explanation: They have their own unique cultures, traditions and languages.

questions.question: Why are Adivasis often marginalised?
questions.correct_answer: Because of displacement from forests, lack of access to education and economic opportunities.
questions.explanation: Development projects and loss of forest resources affect their livelihoods.

questions.question: Who are minorities?
questions.correct_answer: Minorities are groups of people who are smaller in number compared to the rest of the population.
questions.explanation: They may differ in religion, language or culture.

questions.question: Which report studied the condition of Muslims in India?
questions.explanation: It examined the social, economic and educational conditions of Muslims in India.

questions.question: What are stereotypes?
questions.correct_answer: Stereotypes are fixed ideas or beliefs about a particular group of people.
questions.explanation: They often lead to unfair judgments and discrimination.

questions.question: What is prejudice?
questions.correct_answer: Prejudice is judging people unfairly without knowing the facts.
questions.explanation: It can lead to discrimination and marginalisation.

questions.question: How does marginalisation affect people?
questions.correct_answer: It limits access to education, employment, healthcare and political participation.

questions.question: How can marginalisation be reduced?
questions.correct_answer: By promoting equality, education and policies that protect disadvantaged groups.
questions.explanation: Government programs and laws can help improve opportunities.

questions.question: Why is equality important in society?
questions.correct_answer: Equality ensures that all people have the same rights and opportunities.
questions.explanation: It helps create a fair and just society.

notes.title: Meaning of Marginalisation
notes.content: Marginalisation refers to social exclusion where certain groups have fewer opportunities and resources.

notes.title: Adivasis
notes.content: Adivasis are indigenous communities who depend on forests and natural resources for their livelihood.

notes.title: Minorities
notes.content: Minorities are smaller groups in society that may differ by religion, language or culture.

notes.title: Stereotypes and Prejudice
notes.content: Stereotypes and prejudice can lead to discrimination against communities.

notes.title: Effects of Marginalisation
notes.content: Marginalisation can affect education, employment and social participation.

facts.fact: Adivasis form about 8% of India's population.
The Sachar Committee Report studied the condition of Muslims in India.
Stereotypes and prejudice often lead to discrimination.
Equality and inclusion help reduce marginalisation in society.
